news.headline: ClearPoint Neuro outlines growth ambitions in precision neurosurgery
news.summary: Management reiterated its multi-year roadmap across navigation, therapy delivery and its expanding biologics/drug-delivery services ecosystem. The messaging supports the reaffirmed FY2026 revenue guidance of $52-56M and reinforces the razor-and-blade model of hardware placements plus recurring per-procedure consumables.

news.headline: ClearPoint executives (CEO, CBO, CFO) add ESPP shares via Form 4
news.summary: Multiple senior officers acquired shares through the employee stock purchase plan in early July, a modest insider-alignment signal following the mid-June AMT-130 rally. The same window also saw one director-level disposition (~$388K), so the net insider read is constructive but mixed rather than uniformly bullish.

news.headline: Lake Street maintains ClearPoint Neuro at Buy, raises target to $30
news.summary: Lake Street reaffirmed Buy and set what is currently the Street-high $30 target, framing ClearPoint as the primary supplier beneficiary of uniQure's AMT-130 progress. It reflects growing analyst confidence after the FDA news, though it leaves the stock's valuation heavily dependent on partner execution.

news.headline: uniQure plans Q3 2026 BLA for AMT-130 after FDA accepts 3-year data for accelerated approval
news.summary: In a Type B meeting the FDA said the 3-year Phase I/II analysis is acceptable as the primary basis for a BLA under accelerated approval for AMT-130 in Huntington's disease, with submission planned for Q3 2026. AMT-130 is delivered directly into the brain via ClearPoint's SmartFlow cannula and navigation platform, so CLPT benefits directly as the delivery partner. Both stocks surged, with CLPT up roughly 26-32% intraday - the single biggest catalyst of the period.

news.headline: ClearPoint enters 10-year focused-ultrasound partnership with Sungkyunkwan University, South Korea
news.summary: The decade-long collaboration develops focused ultrasound for blood-brain-barrier opening, expanding ClearPoint's drug-delivery ecosystem and deepening its footprint in Asia. It is a strategic, long-dated bet that broadens the addressable market beyond MRI-guided direct injection, with limited near-term revenue impact.

news.headline: Q1 2026 results: record $12.1M revenue (+43% YoY), FY guide $52-56M; Velocity Alpha MR drill earns CE mark
news.summary: Record quarterly revenue of about $12.1M grew 43% year over year (16% organic, boosted by IRRAflow), though revenue and EPS of $(0.32) missed estimates. Management reaffirmed FY2026 revenue guidance of $52-56M with an installed base now above 175 centers, and the CE-marked Velocity Alpha MR high-speed surgical drill (later used clinically for the first time) extended the neurosurgery hardware portfolio.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings (after market close)
catalysts.detail: Next quarterly report; watch organic growth, IRRAflow integration, cash burn and confirmation of the $52-56M FY guide. Date follows the historical mid-August cadence and is not yet formally confirmed.

catalysts.label: uniQure AMT-130 BLA submission
catalysts.detail: uniQure plans to file the BLA for AMT-130 in Huntington's under accelerated approval as early as Q3/October 2026; ClearPoint's SmartFlow cannula is the delivery device, making this the key partner milestone.

catalysts.label: Potential FDA decision on AMT-130
catalysts.detail: Assuming a Q3 2026 filing and rolling review, an FDA decision could arrive around mid-2027; approval of a first Huntington's gene therapy would establish recurring per-procedure delivery revenue for ClearPoint.

catalysts.label: Revenue guidance $52-56M
catalysts.detail: Management's full-year target implies continued double-digit growth; meeting or raising it is viewed as a share-price catalyst.

catalysts.label: Velocity Alpha drill / navigation rollout
catalysts.detail: Continued commercialization of the CE-marked, FDA-cleared Velocity Alpha MR drill and navigation systems (including the Health Canada license) into new hospital sites to drive hardware and consumable placements.

pulse: CLPT's setup improved markedly over the last 45 days, driven almost entirely by partner uniQure's mid-June regulatory win: the FDA's acceptance of 3-year AMT-130 data for an accelerated-approval BLA (targeted Q3 2026) sent the stock up 26-32% and revived the platform bull case. Analysts are predominantly bullish (Lake Street Street-high at $30), yet the operating picture is mixed - record Q1 revenue and 43% growth, but a top- and bottom-line miss and an unprofitable, partner-dependent model. The biggest upside remains optionality on gene therapies reaching the market rather than standalone fundamentals. Sentiment is constructive and momentum-positive, with modest executive ESPP buying, though a director sale and the multi-year path to an actual AMT-130 approval temper enthusiasm. Near term, the August 11 Q2 print and confirmation of the uniQure BLA filing are the catalysts to watch; between partner headlines the stock tends to consolidate.
